content.problemStatement:
Identify and correct the error in the following code (in the IDE).


Output Format
19
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (0 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. No further data follows.
content.outputFormat: Print the integer 19 on a separate line for each test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are 3 test cases; the program prints 19 for each one.
A single test case results in one line containing 19.
When T = 0, no output is produced.

solution.approach: The task does not require any computation besides reading the number of test cases and printing a constant value. Read T, then repeat T times: output 19 followed by a newline. This runs in linear time with respect to T and uses constant extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
